title: Bridge Essay: Ecological∼Enactivism Through the Lens of Japanese Philosophy.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.

**Ecological∼Enactivism Through the Lens of Japanese Philosophy.** — Front Psychol. PMID: 32903774. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2020.01347. The enactive and ecological approaches to embodied cognitive science are on acollision course. While both draw inspiration from similar views in psychology and phenomenology, the two approaches initially held seemingly contradictory views and points of focus. Early enactivists saw value in the ecological approach but insisted that the two schools remain distinct.
